author: claude
content:
Network-reliability assessment (Stefan + analysis, 2026-06-20): LOW risk of actually suffering. Volume trivial — 24GB/100k sites = ~240KB/site; ~1.2GB/volunteer over a multi-hour/day crawl = one ~240KB commit per ~10-30s per client. The only real failure mode is transient home-network disconnects, which degrade to RETRIES (not data loss) given (a) the StorageController commit-or-requeue forward-progress guarantee (worker in progress) + (b) a reconnect-with-backoff wrapper on the DB connection. No edge buffering needed -> reinforces the direct-to-Postgres decision.

PER-CLIENT SHARDING (Stefan): generalize the parquet provider's per-instance sharding (arrow_storage.py:42,59 — self._instance_id = random.getrandbits(32), stamped on every record for partitioning) to the SQLAlchemy provider: add an instance_id column to every table, set per provider instance. Buys per-client isolation (no cross-client lock contention) AND fixes the per-node-sequential visit_id/browser_id collision across volunteers in one move. QUEUED to run on #1161 after the current deps+DSN-wiring worker lands (avoid same-branch push race).
